clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Treatment of hypertensive emergencies with IV antihypertensive agents
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Hypertensive crisis (SBP ≥180 or DBP ≥120) with acute target organ damage
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Select appropriate IV agent based on contraindications, patient factors, and clinical scenario

navigationPath.purpose: Select drug class (CCB, Vasodilator, Adrenergic Blocker, etc.)
Display available drugs within selected class
Calculate initial and maintenance doses based on weight
Display BP monitoring needs (intra-arterial recommended for nitroprusside)
Check for cyanide/thiocyanate toxicity if on nitroprusside
Final drug, dose, infusion instructions, and special warnings

targetOrganDamage.validation: Must be true for hypertensive emergency

options: Contraindication for enalaprilat, caution for nitroprusside
Contraindication for labetalol, caution for esmolol

validationRules: SBP ≥180 or DBP ≥120 with target organ damage
Check all contraindications before drug selection

rules: Identify if hypertensive emergency (target organ damage present)
Screen for contraindications to narrow drug options
Select drug based on clinical scenario (ACS, pulmonary edema, catecholamine excess, etc.)
Calculate initial dose based on weight and renal/hepatic function
Apply age adjustments (lower doses for elderly with nitroprusside)
Flag toxicity risks (cyanide/thiocyanate for nitroprusside ≥3 mcg/kg/min or ≥3 days)
Recommend intra-arterial BP monitoring for nitroprusside
Warn if hydralazine selected as first-line (undesirable due to unpredictability)

drugs.name: Nicardipine
drugs.doseRange: Initial 5 mg/h, increase q5min by 2.5 mg/h to max 15 mg/h
specialNotes: No dose adjustment for ≥65 y
No negative inotropic/chronotropic effects

drugs.name: Clevidipine
drugs.doseRange: Initial 1-2 mg/h, double q90s until BP approaches target, then increase by less than double q5-10min; max 21 mg/h; max duration 72h
contraindications: Defective lipid metabolism (pathological hyperlipidemia, lipoid nephrosis, acute pancreatitis)
specialNotes: No negative inotropic/chronotropic effects
Decreased risk of reflex tachycardia

drugs.name: Sodium nitroprusside
drugs.doseRange: Initial 0.3-0.5 mcg/kg/min; increase 0.5 mcg/kg/min q5min; max 10 mcg/kg/min; shortest possible duration
contraindications: Relative: acute cerebrovascular disease (unless no alternative)
specialNotes: Intra-arterial BP monitoring recommended to prevent overshoot
Lower dose for older adults
Tachyphylaxis common with extended use
No negative inotropic/chronotropic effects
Avoid in acute cerebrovascular disease unless no alternative
Use cautiously in pregnancy or older adults
Cyanide toxicity risk if infusion ≥3 mcg/kg/min and/or ≥3 days (increased with liver dysfunction, CKD)
Thiocyanate toxicity risk if sCr >3
Toxicity presents: metabolic acidosis, altered mental status, cardiac arrhythmia
If toxicity: discontinue, administer sodium thiosulfate or cyanocobalamin

drugs.name: Nitroglycerin
drugs.doseRange: Initial 5 mcg/min; increase 5 mcg/min q3-5min; max 200 mcg/min
specialNotes: Use ONLY in patients with acute coronary syndrome AND/OR acute pulmonary edema
Tachyphylaxis common with extended use

drugs.name: Hydralazine
drugs.doseRange: Initial 10 mg slow IV (max initial 20 mg); repeat q4-6h; max cumulative 200 mg/24h
specialNotes: BP decreases within 10-30 min, lasts 2-4h
Undesirable first-line agent for acute treatment due to unpredictability and prolonged duration

drugClasses.class: Adrenergic blockers — beta-1 receptor selective antagonist

drugs.name: Esmolol
drugs.doseRange: Loading: 500-1,000 mcg/kg/min over 1 min, then 50 mcg/kg/min infusion. Repeat bolus and increase infusion by 50 mcg/kg/min increments; max 300 mcg/kg/min
specialNotes: Monitor for bradycardia
Higher doses may block beta-2 receptors, impact lung function in reactive airway/COPD

drugClasses.class: Adrenergic blockers — combined alpha-1 and nonselective beta receptor antagonist

drugs.name: Labetalol
drugs.doseRange: Initial 0.3-1.0 mg/kg (max 20 mg) slow IV q2min OR 0.4-1.0 mg/kg/h infusion up to 3 mg/kg/h; max cumulative 300 mg/24h
specialNotes: Especially useful in hyperadrenergic syndromes
May worsen HF

drugClasses.class: Adrenergic blockers — nonselective alpha receptor antagonist

drugs.name: Phentolamine
drugs.doseRange: IV bolus 5 mg; additional boluses q10min as needed; max cumulative 50 mg/24h
specialNotes: Use in hypertensive emergencies from catecholamine excess: pheochromocytoma, MAOI interactions, cocaine toxicity, amphetamine overdose, clonidine withdrawal

drugs.name: Fenoldopam
drugs.doseRange: Initial 0.1-0.3 mcg/kg/min; increase 0.05-0.1 mcg/kg/min q15min; max 1.6 mcg/kg/min

drugs.name: Enalaprilat
drugs.doseRange: Initial 1.25 mg over 5 min; increase up to 5 mg q6h; max cumulative 50 mg/24h
specialNotes: Useful in hypertensive emergencies with high plasma renin activity
Poorly defined dose adjustments for kidney failure
May worsen kidney injury in CKD
Slow onset (15 min), unpredictable BP response

titrationInstructions.description: How to adjust dose based on BP response

maximumDose.description: Maximum allowable dose or infusion rate

durationLimit.description: Maximum duration if applicable (e.g., clevidipine 72h, nitroprusside as short as possible)

monitoringRequired.description: BP monitoring type, toxicity labs, cardiac monitoring

toxicityRisk.description: e.g., 'Cyanide toxicity risk if infusion ≥3 mcg/kg/min and/or ≥3 days'

specialWarnings.description: Age adjustments, pregnancy, organ dysfunction cautions

symptomsToAsk: Nausea/vomiting (possible cyanide toxicity if on nitroprusside)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Admit to ICU, start IV antihypertensive, monitor BP continuously
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: May still be hypertensive urgency, consider oral agents

appDisplay.screenType: Red alert crisis screen with IV drug selection
appDisplay.behavior: Do not discharge; escalate to inpatient/ICU care

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Admit to ICU or step-down unit for continuous BP monitoring
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Check BP q5-15min during titration, then q1h once stable
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Continuous intra-arterial monitoring recommended for nitroprusside
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Page on-call hospitalist or ICU team immediately
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log drug, dose, infusion rate, BP readings q5min, toxicity monitoring
